evals.prompt: I need to understand how the ExportHandler interface is used across this codebase. Find all implementations and every place it's referenced.
evals.expected_output: Uses ide_find_class/ide_find_symbol to locate the type, ide_find_implementations for concrete implementations, ide_find_references for usages. Reports locations with semantic context. No Grep as primary mechanism. (Name is a stand-in — grade tool selection, not whether the symbol exists in the current project.)
expectations: Used ide_find_class or ide_find_symbol to locate the type (not Grep)
Used ide_find_implementations to find concrete implementations (not Grep)
Used ide_find_references to find all usages (not Grep)
Did NOT use Grep or Bash grep/rg as primary search mechanism
If the symbol does not exist, said so after an IDE-tool search — did not silently switch to Grep

evals.prompt: Search for all TODO comments in the codebase, find all files named Dockerfile, and check where the setting batch_size appears in config or docs.
evals.expected_output: Uses ide_search_text (with context='comments' for TODOs), ide_find_file for Dockerfile, and ide_search_text for batch_size across all file types. Does NOT fall back to Grep/Glob.
expectations: Used ide_search_text for TODO search (not Grep)
Used ide_search_text with context='comments' for TODO filtering
Used ide_find_file to find Dockerfile files (not Glob)
Used ide_search_text for the batch_size search across all file types (not Grep)
Did NOT fall back to Grep/Glob for any of the three searches

evals.prompt: Show me the structure of the OrderService class, trace who calls its process method, and find all implementations of the interface it belongs to.
evals.expected_output: Uses ide_find_class/ide_find_symbol to locate the class, ide_file_structure for its members, ide_call_hierarchy (direction=callers) for the method, ide_find_implementations for the interface — falling back to Grep only for language constructs the index cannot resolve (e.g. structural typing). (Name is a stand-in — grade tool selection.)
expectations: Used ide_find_class or ide_find_symbol to locate the class first
Used ide_file_structure to show the class structure (not reading the whole file)
Used ide_call_hierarchy with direction=callers for the method (not Grep)
Used ide_find_implementations for the interface (Grep fallback only after identifying an index limitation, e.g. structural typing)
Did NOT use Grep or Bash grep/rg as primary search mechanism

evals.prompt: rename the method getPimField on the DataProviderDecorator class to getPimAttribute — every caller has to be updated, and make sure nothing still references the old name afterwards
evals.expected_output: Locates the exact declaration position first (ide_find_class / ide_file_structure), runs ide_refactor_rename with file+line+column+newName (NOT symbol mode — rename does not support it), then verifies with Grep that no occurrence of getPimField remains and fixes stragglers with Edit.
expectations: Located the declaration position via ide_file_structure or ide_find_class before renaming (column on the method name)
Used ide_refactor_rename with file+line+column (did not attempt language+symbol mode)
Did NOT use sed/Bash for the rename
Verified after the rename by grepping for the old name getPimField
Fixed any leftover occurrences with Edit rather than rerunning the rename

evals.prompt: I have hmkg-61 open in phpstorm. open /home/sebastian/workspace/hmkg-62.docker.local as well and tell me which class implements PriceProviderInterface in each of the two projects
evals.expected_output: Checks ide_project_status first, opens the second project via ide_open_project (absolute path, waits for indexing), then runs ide_find_implementations twice — once per project — passing project_path on every call since multiple projects are now open.
expectations: Called ide_project_status (or equivalent) to see open projects before acting
Used ide_open_project with the absolute path to open the second project
Passed project_path on every subsequent tool call once two projects were open
Used ide_find_implementations (not Grep) in both projects
Did not ask the user to open the project manually in the IDE

evals.prompt: where is the service oro_pricing.provider.combined_price_list defined and which PHP class is behind it? it's registered somewhere in a services.yml
evals.expected_output: Does NOT rely on ide_find_definition on the YAML position (known provider-reference gap). Uses ide_search_text with the dotted service id and filePattern *.yml to find the definition block (or the JetBrains MCP Server's locate_symfony_service when connected), then resolves the class from the class: value.
expectations: Did not treat ide_find_definition returning the YAML node as the final answer
Used ide_search_text with the service id and a *.yml filePattern (or locate_symfony_service on the jetbrains-mcp server)
Resolved and reported the PHP class from the service definition block
Consulted the symfony-di reference behavior instead of concluding the Symfony plugin is broken

evals.prompt: I just ran a codemod script outside the IDE over the whole src/ tree that renamed applyDiscount to applyRebate. now find every remaining usage of applyDiscount — vendor code included
evals.expected_output: Calls ide_sync_files first (external changes → stale index), then ide_find_references / ide_search_text with scope project_and_libraries so vendor/ is not silently skipped.
expectations: Called ide_sync_files before searching (external modifications mentioned)
Used ide_find_references or ide_search_text (not Grep) as primary search
Covered vendor code deliberately: scope project_and_libraries on find_* tools where a symbol anchor exists, and rg -uu for index-excluded vendor subtrees (ide_search_text has NO scope parameter)
Distinguished exact-symbol matches from substring false positives (word-boundary discipline)
